# Date Range Picker

Selects an inclusive start and end date from a two-month popover.

## Installation

Add Date Range Picker from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/date-range-picker
```

## Usage

Date Range Picker is a StackTrace component for a compact, date-only interval. Bind the endpoints separately with `v-model:from` and `v-model:until`. Both models are ISO date strings or `undefined`.

**Date range picker example**

```vue
<template>
  <DateRangePicker
    v-model:from="from"
    v-model:until="until"
    class="w-[280px]"
  />
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { DateRangePicker } from '@/Components/DateRangePicker'

const from = ref<string>()
const until = ref<string>()
</script>
```

## Range model

The component parses each endpoint with `parseDate` and emits changes independently. A first click can therefore set `from` before `until` exists. Treat the endpoints as an inclusive calendar range, and decide explicitly whether the form may submit an incomplete range. Do not pass localized display strings, JavaScript `Date` objects, or date-time values.

## Display and time zone

The trigger shows a range only after both endpoints are present. The local wrapper uses a fixed `en-US` medium-date formatter and the browser's local time zone for display conversion. The stored ISO endpoints remain zone-free calendar dates. Locale and time-zone configuration are not exposed by this wrapper.

## Layout and portal

The popover always renders two months. Verify that the layout fits at narrow breakpoints. Pass `to` as a selector or element when the content needs a different portal root; width classes on the component style the trigger.

## Validation and accessibility

Use a visible field label that describes the interval, such as “Stay dates,” rather than labeling each endpoint separately when the picker acts as one control. The packaged wrapper does not expose an id or accessible name prop for its internal trigger; compose the lower-level parts when explicit label association is required. Validate that both values exist, that `from` is not after `until`, and that the inclusive duration satisfies product rules. Reka supplies range-calendar grid navigation and popover focus behavior; do not replace it with pointer-only handlers.

The packaged wrapper does not expose minimum, maximum, unavailable dates, maximum range length, or non-contiguous range settings. Compose Range Calendar directly when users must be prevented from choosing those invalid ranges.

## Choosing a range control

> **Use Range Calendar for full control: [Range Calendar](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/range-calendar)**
>
> Use Range Calendar when the grid should remain visible or the product needs bounds, unavailable dates, locale control, or range-length rules.

> **Use Date Picker for one day: [Date Picker](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/date-picker)**
>
> If the workflow needs only one date, Date Picker avoids the partial state and wider two-month overlay.

Hosted documentation: [Date Range Picker](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/date-range-picker)
